title: Rewilding
description: For questions 9 – 16, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only one word in each gap. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: Rewilding is (0) a conservation approach that aims to restore natural ecosystems by reducing human intervention and allowing nature to take care of itself. This strategy has gained popularity as an alternative to traditional conservation methods.||The concept involves bringing back native species that have (9) been extinct in an area for many years, including large predators and herbivores. These keystone species play crucial roles (10) in maintaining ecological balance. For example, when wolves were reintroduced to Yellowstone National Park in the 1990s, they controlled deer populations, allowing vegetation (11) to recover. This in turn benefited numerous other species, demonstrating (12) how interconnected ecosystems truly are.||Rewilding projects differ (13) both in scale and scope, from small nature reserves to vast wilderness areas. Some focus on reintroducing specific species, while others concentrate (14) on removing barriers like dams or fences to allow natural processes to resume. In Europe, abandoned farmland is being allowed to return to forest, creating habitats for wildlife that had disappeared from these areas.||Critics worry (15) about potential conflicts between rewilded areas and farming communities, particularly when predators are involved. There are also debates about which historical period should (16) be used as a reference point for restoration. Despite these challenges, rewilding offers hope for restoring biodiversity and natural ecosystem functions in an increasingly human-dominated world.

example.text: Rewilding is (0) conservation approach that aims to restore natural ecosystems.

questions.explanation: Present perfect: 'have been extinct' indicates a persisting state.
Expression 'play roles in' indicates participation in a process.
Expression 'allowing vegetation to recover' with an infinitive.
Adverbio interrogativo 'how' introduce pregunta indirecta sobre el grado.
Used as part of the correlative conjunction 'both... and', indicating that two things are equally included.
Verb 'concentrate on' means to focus on something specific.
Expression 'worry about' introduces the topic of concern.
Modal passive voice: 'should be used' expresses a recommendation.
